name: feat-11-rate-limiting
initialPrompt: We've had two incidents where a single customer hammered the API and degraded service for everyone else. I need rate limiting on our public API: per-API-key limits for standard operations, stricter limits on expensive endpoints (search, export, bulk operations), clear 429 responses with Retry-After headers, a self-service rate-limit status page for customers, and an internal dashboard for ops to view current usage and temporarily raise limits. Also add alerting when a key is consistently hitting its limit.

cannedAuthorResponse.title: API rate limiting with customer visibility

blocks.text: Protect API stability under heavy load while giving customers clear visibility into their usage
successCriteria: no single key can degrade service for others
ops can raise limits without a code deploy

blocks.question: Where do we store rate-limit counters?
blocks.rationale: In-process counters break under horizontal scaling; Redis gives accurate cross-instance counts.

blocks.title: Implement core rate-limiter middleware (Redis sliding window)
acceptance: per-API-key limit enforced
429 returned with Retry-After and X-RateLimit-* headers
limit configurable per key without deploy

blocks.title: Configure tiered limits for expensive endpoints (search, export, bulk)
acceptance: search endpoint has separate, lower limit
export and bulk endpoints each have their own tier
tiers documented in API docs

blocks.title: Build self-service rate-limit status page for customers
acceptance: shows current window usage vs limit per tier
resets-at timestamp displayed
accessible from API key settings page

blocks.title: Build internal ops dashboard for usage monitoring and limit overrides
acceptance: top-N consumers shown in real-time
ops can set temporary limit override per key
override expires automatically

blocks.description: Redis unavailability would disable rate limiting entirely, allowing unconstrained traffic
blocks.mitigation: Fail open with conservative in-process fallback; alert on Redis connectivity loss

blocks.question: What should the default rate limits be for standard, search, and bulk tiers? Needs input from product and sales.

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same risk (Redis as SPOF for rate limiting) but description and mitigation expanded to cover HA planning; id renamed to better reflect scope. AC-13 fallback must map risk-redis-single-point to risk-redis-failover.

decoy.note: Genuinely-new block. Shares surface tokens 'limit', 'ops', and 'key' with task-ops-dashboard. A comment on task-ops-dashboard must re-attach to task-ops-dashboard (id preserved), NOT this new alerting task.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-spike-redis-perf inserted second (shifts all subsequent positions); task-limit-breach-alerting added; risk-redis-single-point id changed to risk-redis-failover. Naive positional scheme loses alignment at dec-rate-limit-store onward. Denominator is 7.
